idea.hook: Light is fast. REALLY fast. But...
facts: Light travels at an incredible 299,792,458 meters per second.
It's so fast, it can circle Earth 7.5 times in just ONE second!
But even at this speed, it takes 8 minutes to reach us from the Sun (150 million km), and 4.2 YEARS to reach the nearest star, Proxima Centauri.
idea.payoff: The universe's true scale makes even light speed feel like standing still.
idea.title: 🤯 The INSANE Speed of Light... Isn't Enough!

scenes.text: Light is fast. REALLY fast. But...
scenes.visual: A dark, deep space background with subtle, shimmering light trails appearing and fading.
A numerical counter rapidly displaying digits, settling on the final speed. A thin light beam pulses in the background.

scenes.text: Circles Earth 7.5x in just ONE second!
scenes.visual: An animated Earth model, with a glowing light streak rapidly circling it 7.5 times within the duration.

scenes.text:
Sun: 8 min. (150M km)
Proxima Centauri: 4.2 YEARS!
scenes.visual: Scene transitions from a bright sun with Earth nearby, then quickly zooms out to show the vast distance to a tiny distant star, emphasizing scale with light beams traveling.

scenes.text: The universe makes even light speed feel like standing still.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, slowly zooming out view of a massive galaxy cluster or deep field image, showcasing the immense scale of the cosmos.
